## Summary

Describe the purpose of this pull request and the main changes it introduces.

## Related Issues

Link related issues using keywords such as `Closes #123` or `Relates to #123`.

## Change Type

Select all that apply:

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor or maintenance change

## Testing

Describe the testing you completed for this change.

- [ ] Added or updated automated tests where appropriate
- [ ] Ran the relevant test suite locally
- [ ] Verified the change manually where automated coverage is not practical
- [ ] Confirmed all CI checks pass

## Documentation

- [ ] Updated relevant documentation, comments, or examples
- [ ] No documentation changes are needed

## Dependencies

List any dependencies introduced, removed, or updated by this change. If none,
state `None`.

## Reviewer Notes

Call out any areas where you would like specific feedback, any known limitations,
or any follow-up work that is intentionally out of scope.

## Contributor Checklist

- [ ] I have read and followed the project's contribution guidelines
- [ ] My changes follow the existing code style and conventions
- [ ] I have included tests or explained why tests are not needed
- [ ] I have updated documentation where relevant
- [ ] I have rebased or merged the latest `main` branch changes

## Maintainer Review Guidance

Before approving or merging, maintainers should confirm that:

- The pull request scope is clear and matches the summary
- Related issues are linked and resolved appropriately
- Tests and CI results support the change
- Documentation is updated when user-facing behavior changes
- Any dependency changes are justified and safe
- Breaking changes are clearly documented and communicated
